title: Senior Flutter Engineer
description:
**About us** 




 Avy is transforming the housing experience by enabling property companies to provide a modern, seamless service for their residents. Our platform helps reduce operational costs, create new revenue streams, and enhance tenant satisfaction - all through an intuitive digital experience.
 



 For over six years, Avy has been shaping the digital housing experience together with its customers, partners, and employees. As the leading Tenant Experience platform in the Nordics, we reach more than 500,000 households - approximately 20% of all tenants in Sweden. Our mission is to push boundaries and redefine what it means to live in a rental home - making it more sustainable, seamless, and secure.
 



  





**Role Description** 




 As a
 **Senior Flutter Engineer** 
 at Avy, you will play a pivotal role in designing and building engaging, high-performing web applications that enhance the living experience for thousands of users. You will work closely with our product and design teams to craft intuitive and accessible interfaces, ensuring seamless interactions between our platform, tenants and property owners. With a strong foundation in modern frontend technologies and a passion for user experience, you will drive the evolution of our platform while contributing to best practices, scalability, and performance optimization.
 



  





 This role requires deep technical expertise, a problem-solving mindset, and a desire to impact the future of digital living.
 



  





 Strong requirement for this position is fluency in Swedish language.
 



  





**Responsibilities** 



* Engineer and implement front-end for Avy
* Develop secure, clean and scalable code
* Make sure that written code is covered by tests with defined acceptance levels
* Work closely with other technical team members (front-end and back-end) in active discussions around solution and everyday work
* Work closely with design and product team in agile and scrum process
* Work closely with Product Owner in roadmap creation process
* Open to work in high paced, stable and agile start-up environment



  





 As this is a Senior role, you need to have at least several years of experience in Flutter development. (yes, we know Flutter is fairly new)
 



  





**Qualifications** 



* Previous experience in Flutter
* Previous experience with react-native or Android/iOS native development
* Material Design and Apple Human Interface Guidelines
* 5+ years experience with JavaScript or JAVA or Swift
* Experience consuming (non-)RESTful APIs
* Structured analytical mindset with strong communication skills
* **Experience leading teams**



  





**Perks and benefits** 



* Team: Brilliant colleagues and interesting challenges
* Location: Creative office space in the Stockholm centre
* Salary: Competitive salary and compensation
* Language: English or Swedish



  





**Location:** 



* Stockholm, Sweden



  





 \*\*EU Candidates Only - No Relocation Provided at the moment\*\*
 



  





 \*\* For this position, Avy is working with in-house hiring team and we are not accepting external partners or agencies \*\*

title: Affärsutvecklare Fastighet
description:
**Är du en affärsutvecklare med teknisk förståelse för fastigheter?**




 Trivs du med att skapa nya affärer, bygga relationer och utveckla konkurrenskraftiga lösningar? Vi söker dig som vill kombinera affärer och fastighetsteknik och vara med och utveckla Coors affär inom tekniska fastighetstjänster.
   

  





 Hos oss får du möjligheten att arbeta i en av Nordens ledande FM- och fastighetsorganisationer där du får stort eget ansvar, möjlighet att påverka affären och vara en del av en tillväxtresa inom Affärsområde Fastighet.
   

  





 Välkommen till Coor – hos oss är du skillnaden.
   

  





  





**Om rollen**




 Som affärsutvecklare inom Affärsområde Fastighet blir du en viktig del av vår fortsatta tillväxt och affärsutveckling. Du arbetar med försäljning av tekniska fastighetstjänster till kunder inom både privat och offentlig sektor.
   

  





 Tyngdpunkten i rollen ligger på uppsökande försäljning, där du aktivt identifierar, bearbetar och utvecklar nya affärsmöjligheter. Genom ett systematiskt och målinriktat säljarbete bygger du upp en stark pipeline av potentiella kunder och driver affärsdialoger. Med din tekniska förståelse och affärsmässighet hjälper du kunder att hitta effektiva, hållbara och långsiktigt värdeskapande lösningar för sina fastigheter.
   

  





 Rollen har ett tydligt fokus på affärsutveckling och anbudsproduktion. Du driver affärsprocessen från första kontakt till färdigt anbud och arbetar nära verksamhetens specialister för att säkerställa attraktiva, genomförbara och lönsamma lösningar.
   

  





  





**Exempel på arbetsuppgifter**




 ·Identifiera och utveckla nya affärsmöjligheter
   

  





 ·Bearbeta och etablera nya kundrelationer
   

  





 ·Leda och samordna anbudsprocesser
   

  





 ·Utveckla kundanpassade lösningar inom teknisk fastighetsdrift och FM
   

  





 ·Delta i upphandlingar, förhandlingar och kundpresentationer
   

  





 ·Bidra till den strategiska utvecklingen av Affärsområde Fastighets erbjudanden
   

  





  





 Vi söker tre affärsutvecklare med placering i Stockholm, Göteborg eller Malmö.
   





**Vi söker dig som vill kombinera affärer och fastighetsteknik**




 Du drivs av att skapa affärer, utveckla relationer och se resultat av ditt arbete. Samtidigt har du förståelse för de tekniska utmaningar och möjligheter som finns inom fastighetsbranschen.
   

  





  





**Du är:**




 ·Resultatinriktad, självgående och ansvarstagande
   

  





 ·Orädd och trivs med att skapa nya kundkontakter
   

  





 ·Affärsmässig och skicklig på att identifiera kunders behov
   

  





 ·Kommunikativ och uttrycker dig väl på svenska och engelska
   

  





 ·Strukturerad och van att driva affärer från start till mål
   

  





 ·Passionerad kring att bygga långsiktiga relationer
   

  





  





**Vi ser också att du har:**




 ·Flerårig erfarenhet av B2B-försäljning, affärsutveckling och anbudsarbete
   

  





 ·Dokumenterad erfarenhet av att skapa, utveckla och vinna affärer
   

  





 ·B-körkort
   

  





 ·En eftergymnasial utbildning inom teknik, fastighet eller affärsutveckling (meriterande)
   

  





 ·Erfarenhet av komplex tjänsteförsäljning (meriterande)
   

  





  





**För att lyckas i rollen behöver du dessutom:**




 ·Ha en teknisk bakgrund eller flerårig erfarenhet från fastighetsbranschen
   

  





 ·Ha god förståelse för fastighetsdrift, teknisk förvaltning, installation
   

  





 ·Kunna omsätta kunders tekniska och affärsmässiga behov till konkurrenskraftiga lösningar
   

  





 För att lyckas i rollen behöver du förstå fastigheters tekniska funktioner och verksamhetskritiska behov. Därför är erfarenhet från fastighetsbranschen ett krav.
   

  





  





**Varför ska du välja Coor?**




 På Coor får du mer än bara ett jobb. Du får möjlighet att påverka, utvecklas och vara en del av ett företag där människor gör skillnad varje dag.
   

  





 Hos oss får du:
   

  





 ·Arbeta i ett av Nordens ledande FM- och fastighetsbolag
   

  





 ·Vara med och utveckla affärer som skapar verkligt värde för kunder och samhälle
   

  





 ·Frihet under ansvar och stort eget mandat i din roll
   

  





 ·Möjlighet att samarbeta med några av branschens mest erfarna specialister
   

  





 ·Karriär- och utvecklingsmöjligheter inom en stor och växande organisation
   

  





 ·En kultur präglad av samarbete, omtanke och entreprenörskap
   

  





  





**Om tjänsten**



  
* Anställningsform: Tillsvidareanställning
* Omfattning: Heltid
* Placering: Stockholm, Göteborg eller Malmö
* Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse
* Provanställning: 6 månader



 För att säkerställa en rättvis och träffsäker rekryteringsprocess använder vi personlighetstester och kan komma att genomföra bakgrundskontroll inför anställning.
   

  





 Vi behandlar personuppgifter enligt GDPR, tar endast emot ansökningar via vårt rekryteringssystem och fattar alla urvalsbeslut genom mänsklig bedömning.
   

  





  





**Övrig information**




 Har du frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Per Stinessen, Chef Growth & Retention, per.stinessen@coor.com
   

  





 Urval och intervjuer påbörjas i mitten av augusti.
   

  





  





 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!

title: Audit Data & AI Solutions Engineer
description:
**Role Summary**
 TRATON Financial Services’ Internal Audit department is looking for an Audit Data & AI Solutions Engineer to help build the next generation of audit technology.
   

  

 Internal Audit is the third line of defense. We look for things others may have missed — the proverbial needle in the haystack. Our role is to assess whether controls designed to prevent mistakes, fraud, inefficiency, and regulatory breaches are actually working in practice.
   

  

 For several years, we have successfully used source-system data, analytics, and dashboards to make audits more targeted and data-driven. Building on that foundation, we are now expanding into AI-enabled audit technology: automating parts of audit sample testing by drawing evidence-based conclusions from documents in multiple languages, identifying unusual assets or transactions, and building dashboards and applications that help auditors focus on what matters most.
   

  

 You will be part of a small team within Internal Audit that builds the solutions to make this happen. The work spans hands-on data mapping, source-system analysis, Power BI or HTML dashboards, anomaly detection, machine learning, and agentic AI solutions for audit sample testing and document review.
   

  

 This creates both opportunities and challenges. We can move quickly, test ideas, and learn through short feedback loops with auditors. At the same time, we do not have large predefined frameworks, methods, or roadmaps. We need people who enjoy building things in complex environments, can operate between data, software, AI, and business processes, and can create solutions that are structured, documented, trustworthy, and sustainable.
   

  

**Job Responsibilities**
 As Audit Data & AI Solutions Engineer, you will build and maintain data, analytics, automation, and AI solutions that help Internal Audit assess controls and identify anomalies. That includes:
   

  

  

* Retrieving, mapping, validating, and structuring data from source systems across brands and markets
* Building analytics and algorithms to identify anomalies, control weaknesses, unusual transactions, approval issues, collateral risks, or financial crime indicators
* Developing AI-enabled and agentic solutions for document analysis and audit sample testing, including prompts, skills, tools, agent workflows, evaluation logic, and evidence-based outputs
* Creating Power BI, HTML, or other dashboards that present complex findings in a clear and useful way
* Ensuring that algorithms, prompts, data flows, and assumptions are documented and built in a modular way so they can be reused.


**Who You Are**
 We are looking for a fast learner and hands-on builder who has:
   

  

  

* Strong Python and SQL skills
* Comfort using AI tools and coding agents such as Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, or similar to write, test, debug, and improve code
* Curiosity to understand business processes, controls, audit logic, and what makes an analysis useful in practice
* The ability to turn unclear business problems into working, tested, and maintainable solutions
* Experience building practical technical solutions such as scripts, data tools, dashboards, applications, automations, or analytics workflows
* Strong analytical and debugging skills, including the ability to investigate data quality issues, logic errors, and unexpected results
* A structured approach to documentation, testing, version control, and reusable code
* Excellent communication skills in English, both written and verbal.


 It is an advantage if you have experience with:
   

  

  

* Power BI, data modelling, DAX, Power Query, or code-based Power BI development
* Machine learning, anomaly detection, exception reporting, or process monitoring
* LLM applications, document analysis, agents, RAG, prompt/context engineering, tool use, skills, OCR, JSON, APIs, or workflow applications
* Additional languages, data visualization, financial services, audit, risk, compliance, or controls.


**This Is Us**
 At TRATON Financial Services (TFS) we have started the journey to become the provider of Financial Services to all TRATON brands. Our service includes financing for customers, dealers, and distributors as well as insurance solutions. With a presence in over 65 markets, we are already one of the most global actors in the financial services industry and are rapidly growing. Do you want to be part of this journey?
   

  

**TRATON Financial Services Offers**
 We believe great work starts with feeling supported, both professionally and personally. That’s why we offer a broad range of employee benefits designed to promote wellbeing, flexibility, and a healthy work-life balance. Employees can enjoy flexible working hours and a workplace that supports both collaboration and flexibility. With office Hubs in Södertälje and Stockholm, as well as coworking spaces at Sergels Torg and Kungens Kurva, you will have the best preconditions to pick the best workplace for your activities.
   

  

 We provide a yearly wellness contribution and you can train at our health centre Gröndal, and you will access to occupational health services with doctors, nurses, and ergonomists. Mental health is important to us, therefore we also offer confidential staff support services for guidance related to personal challenges.
   

  

 In addition, employees benefit from pension plans, profit sharing programs, and extra time off or additional pension contributions. We also support family life through enhanced parental benefits and offer additional perks such as employee car leasing. Together, these benefits help create a workplace where you can thrive both at work and in everyday life.
   

  

**Application**
 We look forward to receiving your application, consisting of your CV and kindly ask you not to share a cover letter to ensure an efficient and unbiased recruitment process for all parties.
   

  

 Apply as soon as possible, no later than 2026-07-24. Screening will take place on an ongoing basis during the application period. Technical case, logical and personality tests may be used as part of the selection process, and a background check is required for this role.
   

  

 If you have questions or would like more information, please contact: Mikael Agerbo, Audit Data Analytics Manager, mikael.agerbo@tratonfs.com.
   

  

**We look forward to your application!**
 This recruitment process is handled by Scania for TRATON Financial Services
   

  

 #TFS

title: CONTENT CREATOR
description:
Butterfly Effect specializes in vitalizing brands by harnessing the power of pop culture to create impactful marketing campaigns. We focus on creating engagement through targeted activations in social media, influencer partnerships, and cutting-edge content creating.
   

  

 We Are Looking For a Content Creator With a Proven Track Record Of Producing Viral Videos. You Have a Passion For Pop Culture, a Strong Interest In Internet Culture, And Spend a Lot Of Time On TikTok. Based On Previous Experience, You Also Have
   

  

* Deep understanding of how content goes viral
* Strong insight into user behaviors and platform trends
* Experience in tracking and optimizing content performance


 In this role, you will be a central part of our fast-moving and dynamic work environment. We're seeking someone who is curious, structured, independent, embraces challenges, takes initiative, and has a strong drive.
   

  

 ‍
   

  

**Your Ongoing Tasks Will Include**
* Creating viral content that drives engagement and reach
* Analyzing performance and optimizing content strategies
* Contribute to strategies, creative ideas, and content processes
* Help develop methodologies, tools, and formats that improve operations
* Staying ahead of trends and bringing fresh, pop culture-inspired ideas
* Explore and implement AI tools in your creative workflow


 ‍
   

  

**What’s In It For You**
* Be part of an ambitious Gen Z team
* Work with exciting brands and creative campaigns
* Extensive opportunities for learning and growth
* A chance to help shape the future of influencer marketing


 ‍
   

  

**Location** 
 : Stockholm
   

  

**Type** 
 : Full-time
   

  

 ‍
   

  

 Ready to help us create the next viral moment? Apply now at hello@butterflyeffect.ai
   

  

 Please send your CV along with two case studies showcasing how you have created virality through influencer marketing.

title: Data Center Facilities Technician, Controls
description:
**Minimum qualifications:**
* Associate's degree, trade school certification, or other certified training in a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.
* 5 years of experience in controls/automation in an industrial or commercial environment.
* Ability to participate in material handling tasks such as lifting, carrying or moving up to 50 lbs (23 kg) of equipment, the required use of safety equipment such as limited weight bearing work platforms, ladders, step stools and personal protective equipment (PPE) like safety harnesses in accordance with safety and ergonomic guidelines and assessments.
* Ability to work non-standard hours and differing work rotations/shifts.


**Preferred qualifications:**
* 5 years of experience in construction/technical environment or a related field.
* Experience in data centers, hospitals, power plants, or laboratories.
* Experience and understanding of controls/monitoring systems (BMS, PMS, Scada).
* Knowledge of meters, devices, sensors, relays and troubleshooting utilizing standard hand tools, digital metering, or calibration/diagnostic equipment.
* Ability to communicate with contractors who perform maintenance or upgrade work on the data center systems.
* Scripting language proficiency.


**About The Job**
 The Data Center team designs and operates some of the most sophisticated electrical engineering, mechanical engineering and HVAC systems in the world. Facilities Technicians at Google data centers operate, monitor and support physical facilities conditions. Some of these duties will include heating and cooling of air and water, power supply, generators, UPS systems, electrical distribution and control and monitoring systems. You regularly help inspect, maintain and repair various data center systems such as piping and non-critical electrical or mechanical system components).
   

  

 As an advocate for best practices, you develop creative approaches to reducing operational costs while improving overall data center efficiency. You ensure that environmental and safety standards are consistently met, identifying problems and making repairs quickly. In emergency situations or abnormal conditions, you manage data center performance issues and outages to minimize the recovery time from failures.
   

  

 The Data Center team maintains and operates controls, electrical, mechanical and HVAC systems. Facilities Technicians operate, monitor and maintain critical equipment and physical facilities 24/7 ensuring safe and reliable operations. These systems include heating and cooling of air and water, power supply, generators, UPS systems, electrical distribution and control and monitoring systems. In this role, you will focus is on controls systems such as BMS/PMS/SCADA.
   

  

 Having safe and reliable operations at the core of what we do, you will monitor several data centers from a central control room, ensuring quick response and minimum recovery time from failures. You also troubleshoot problems, perform maintenance and repairs on critical equipment, work collaboratively with internal or external teams to maximize reliability while maintaining a low equipment downtime.
   

  

 Behind everything our users see online is the architecture built by the Technical Infrastructure team to keep it running. From developing and maintaining our data centers to building the next generation of Google platforms, we make Google's product portfolio possible. We're proud to be our engineers' engineers and love voiding warranties by taking things apart so we can rebuild them. We keep our networks up and running, ensuring our users have the best and fastest experience possible.
   

  

 Individual pay is determined by factors including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.
   

  

 Sweden: kr680000 - kr700000 (SEK) + 15% bonus target + equity + benefits
   

  

**Responsibilities**
 Learn more about benefits at Google .
   

  

* Inspect, maintain, and repair various data center systems such as servers, network equipment, PLCs, system administration, access control, alarm investigation, and SCADA management.
* Operate, monitor, maintain, and respond to abnormal conditions in the data center facilities systems and equipment.
* Support startup, commissioning, and integration of new controls equipment and systems into facilities infrastructure.
* Monitor multiple information streams in the control room and make accurate mission-critical decisions.
* Be responsible for the uptime, maintenance, and troubleshooting of building management systems and power monitoring


 Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Technical Deployment Lead, Forward Deployed Engineering - Stockholm
description:
**About The Team**
 OpenAI’s Forward Deployed Engineering team partners with customers to turn research breakthroughs into production systems. We operate at the intersection of customer delivery and core platform development.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Technical Deployment Lead (TDL), you will define how OpenAI delivers complex systems to customers. You will own how they are built, shipped, and adopted. You’ll translate business outcomes into a technical plan, run day-to-day execution across FDEs, Researchers, and Customer Engineers, and partner with customer teams to ensure delivery supports their goals.
   

  

 This is not a management role, however you'll own delivery end-to-end: embedding with customers to map workflows and success criteria, ensuring components ship on time, and leading readiness and change management for adoption. You’ll track progress, manage dependencies, make sequencing decisions, and drive 0→1 prototypes through MVP and scale. You will also share field insights with Product and Research to guide roadmap and priorities.
   

  

 Success will be measured first and foremost by impact - deployments that deliver measurable value against customer goals, drive adoption, and become critical to their workflows. Additional measures of success include delivery reliability (milestones hit, low reopen/churn), operating leverage (patterns reused across deployments), judgment under pressure, and product impact (field signal that shifts roadmaps/architectures).
   

  

 This is a high-trust, high-autonomy role. Success requires deep technical project management expertise, extreme ownership of outcomes, and an ability to immerse in customer workflows and partner with customer teams to solve complex engineering problems at pace.
   

  

 This role is based in Stockholm. We use a hybrid model of 3 days in office and offer relocation assistance. Travel up to 50% is required.
   

  

**In This Role, You Will**
* Own the technical delivery plan for multiple interdependent workstreams. Translate business objectives into a roadmap with milestones, dependencies, and acceptance criteria.
* Run day-to-day engineering execution. Track and drive delivery across OpenAI FDE and customer teams. Keep progress unblocked and sequenced. Make real-time trade-offs on scope and priority to protect the critical path.
* Embed with customer teams to land production deployments and drive adoption. Map workflows, shape tools/integrations, and translate requirements into a delivery plan. Lead onboarding, adoption, and change management.
* Partner with Product and Research so platform components and research workstreams land in time to support deployment goals.
* Codify solution patterns and evals. Extract reusable patterns and package field signals to improve product and models.
* Own value cases and ROI. Set impact hypotheses, baselines, and KPIs; run pre-/post-deployment measurement and report to exec sponsors.


**You’ll Thrive In This Role If You**
* Bring 7+ years of customer‑facing technical delivery leadership.
* Track record of successfully leading large, complex, high-stakes customer engagements where customer outcomes depended on tight coordination and fast decision making, ideally involving AI.
* Excel in high ambiguity environments. Know how to simplify complex and dynamic work.
* Move fluidly between system level understanding and execution level detail; can dive into customer workflows/data, map constraints, sketch architectures and move ambiguous problems to shipped systems.
* Think strategically and pattern-match. Able to step back from execution detail, recognise broader trends across deployments, and connect customer needs to scalable, reusable solutions.
* Have strong technical fluency and sharp sequencing instincts. Confident discussing technical details, pressure-testing architectures, and making trade-offs.
* Have shipped AI/LLM systems. You understand solution patterns, integration basics, and production pitfalls.
* You’re a translator with executive presence. You make complex technical trade-offs legible to business leaders and convert strategy into day-to-day technical execution.
* Enjoy being onsite with customers to accelerate delivery (often 25-50%, sometimes higher).
* Have expertise in at least one major sector (e.g., healthcare, energy, financial services, semiconductors, IT) to elevate solution framing and credibility.


**About OpenAI**
 OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass and value the many different perspectives, voices, and experiences that form the full spectrum of humanity.
   

  

 We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic.
   

  

 For additional information, please see OpenAI’s Affirmative Action and Equal Employment Opportunity Policy Statement.
   

  

 Background checks for applicants will be administered in accordance with applicable law, and qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment consistent with those laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the California Fair Chance Act, for US-based candidates. For unincorporated Los Angeles County workers: we reasonably believe that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of a conditional offer of employment: protect computer hardware entrusted to you from theft, loss or damage; return all computer hardware in your possession (including the data contained therein) upon termination of employment or end of assignment; and maintain the confidentiality of proprietary, confidential, and non-public information. In addition, job duties require access to secure and protected information technology systems and related data security obligations.
   

  

 To notify OpenAI that you believe this job posting is non-compliant, please submit a report through this form. No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance.
   

  

 We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this link.
   

  

 OpenAI Global Applicant Privacy Policy
   

  

 At OpenAI, we believe artificial intelligence has the potential to help people solve immense global challenges, and we want the upside of AI to be widely shared. Join us in shaping the future of technology.

title: SUPPLIER QUALITY DEVELOPMENT ENGINEER (SQDE) TO ÖHLINS
description:
*Öhlins is a Swedish technology company with a global presence and a strong reputation in advanced suspension systems. For 50 years, we’ve been a key player in motorsports and the automotive industry, contributing to over 450 world championship titles.*
*Since January 2025, we’ve been part of the Brembo Group - world leaders in braking solutions- strengthening our innovation capabilities and opening new doors for cutting-edge development.*
*At Öhlins, we put our customers first, delivering solutions that exceed expectations. Our culture is built on passion, collaboration, and a drive to push boundaries. We value trust, sustainability, and long-term partnerships - both within our teams and with partners worldwide.*
*We offer a dynamic, inclusive environment where people from diverse backgrounds come together to shape the future of mobility.*
**ÖHLINS IS NOW LOOKING FOR A SUPPLIER QUALITY DEVELOPMENT ENGINEER (SQDE)**
 We develop and manufacture advanced suspension systems for the future - helping our customers ride faster, safer, and more comfortably in cars, motorcycles and bicycles. The Quality Department is now looking for a new colleague to succeed in our team of Supplier Quality Development Engineers.
   

  

 This position is located at Öhlins' office in Upplands Väsby, north of Stockholm.
   

  

**THE ROLE**
 As a Supplier Quality Development Engineer at Öhlins you will drive improvements in supplier performance, quality, and capability to ensure alignment with company requirements, standards and business objectives. The role involves identifying, evaluating, and auditing suppliers, and lead quality and cost-improvement initiatives. You will develop and implement action plans to secure on-time delivery and high-quality outcomes.
   

  

 Acting as a partner to internal stakeholders, you will build and maintain strong supplier relationships while proactively identifying and mitigating risks within the supply base.
   

  

 You will be a part of a team of four (4) experienced supplier development engineers. You will work together with SQDE colleagues in Japan, Thailand & Taiwan. To achieve our strategic goals, we believe that you will focus on optimizing our processes, improving supplier quality, and driving effective problem-solving to support our own and our customers’ success. The role requires a long-term perspective, initiative, and the ability to make independent decisions by yourself and together with your team.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Drive and enable continuous improvement initiatives.
* Follow up supplier performance and quality results.
* Supplier audits.
* IMDS reporting.
* 8D reporting and handling.
* Approximately 80% mechanics & 20% electronics.


**COMPETENCE PROFILE**
* Audit and supplier quality assurance experience, minimum three (3) years.
* Experience of electronic industry
* Strong communication skills in both Swedish and English, spoken and written
* A strong team-member that motivates, assists and help the team forward.
* Excellent cooperation skills.


 Experience from the manufacturing- and the electronic industry an advantage. We place great emphasis on personal qualities and your ability to independently drive processes forward. You have a structured approach to planning, documentation and following up your work, and you ensure that procedures and standards are followed and continuously developed. You need to be a team-member.
   

  

**Benefits Offered By Öhlins**
 As an employee at Öhlins, you will be part of a supportive and collaborative team where we help each other succeed in our daily work and challenges to continue to build our brand.
   

  

* Subsidized lunches in our on-site staff restaurant.
* Free access to our on-site gym.
* A yearly wellness allowance.
* Work–life balance through flexible working hours and the option to work remotely (up to two days per week), where the nature of the work allows.
* Social and recreational activities, including floorball, ice hockey, golf, ski trips, and track days for cars and motorcycles.
* Employee discounts on our own products.
* Subsidized naprapathy and massage treatments.
* Discounted products and services through the Benifex (formerly Benify) employee benefits portal.


**CONTACT & APPLICATION**
 If you have questions about the position, please contact:
   

  

**Patrik Linde,** 
 Manager Supplier Quality Development Engineering
   

  

 Email: patrik.linde@ohlins.com
   

  

 Phone: +46 (0)76 145 56 24
   

  

 Please submit your application no later than
 **2026-08-30.** 
 The selection will start in the middle of August.
   

  

*To ensure a safe and secure working environment, Öhlins actively promote an alcohol- and drug-free workplace where random alcohol and drug testing is conducted.*

title: Senior Engineer – Systematic Equity
description:
**Who We Are Looking For**
 We are looking for an engineer who can take ownership of the platform that supports our equity investment process. This is a hands-on role that combines engineering with technical leadership. You are expected to write code, but also to take responsibility for how our systems run in production, how they are monitored and operated, and how the team works around them.
   

  

 You have experience with cloud infrastructure and DevOps, and are comfortable making design decisions and setting technical direction within your area of responsibility. While technical experience is essential, we place great importance on personal qualities. We look for individuals who embody our core values: professionalism, humility, generosity, and curiosity. You are pragmatic, collaborative, and take ownership of the work in front of you. AI tools are part of daily engineering at Lynx — we expect you to use them effectively, and to have the judgment to know when their output holds up.
   

  

**Required:**
* Programming skills in Python or similar
* Experience with, or strong interest in, running services in production: monitoring, alerting, observability, and CI/CD
* Experience with cloud infrastructure, preferably AWS


**Meriting:**
* Experience taking technical lead responsibility, formally or informally
* Data engineering experience: ETL, data pipelines, and data quality
* Experience integrating external market data
* Background in finance (ideally systematic trading) or another regulated industry
* Experience setting up production and support routines in a small team


 You are fluent in English and have the right to work in Sweden. The position is based at our office in central Stockholm.
   

  

**About The Role**
 You will join the Systematic Equity team at a formative stage and own the platform our investment process depends on — not just keeping it running, but designing and deciding how it runs. You get the flexibility of a young team with the resources of an established firm behind you. As the strategy scales, so does the role. You will work closely with the researchers in the team, and what you build feeds directly into the investment process.
   

  

 In practice, the core of the role is keeping trading robust and secure: monitoring, alerting, support routines, incident handling, and troubleshooting are your responsibility, as is designing and building the infrastructure that ties the equity systems together.
   

  

 External market data is an important input to the investment process, and the role covers its integration, storage, and validation, including building the tools to monitor and control these flows. You will work on the data pipelines together with the researchers, who depend on solid, high-quality input data for their work.
   

  

 You will also plan and coordinate technical work within the team, across internal stakeholders, and towards external vendors.
   

  

 To apply, please submit your application
 **no later than 17 August.**
**About Us**
 Lynx Asset Management is a quantitative asset manager of around 100 people, based in Stockholm. We apply scientific methods to create systematic investment strategies aimed at providing our clients with high risk-adjusted returns and attractive portfolio characteristics. Innovation has been part of our DNA since we were founded in 1999.
   

  

 At Lynx, we have created a workplace where academic methods and a relentless focus on results are supported by a friendly, collegial culture. The well-being of our employees is especially important to us, as they are the ones who make our success possible. Lynx actively strives to be an inclusive workplace, and we see diversity as a prerequisite for future success.

title: Tech & AI Solutions Lead
description:
**About Areim** 




 Areim is an independent Nordic fund manager, connecting capital, real assets, and expertise. Headquartered in Stockholm, with offices in Copenhagen, Helsinki, Oslo, and London, Areim has invested in real assets since 2003, across sectors including light industrial, data centre, office, logistics, and residential. The firm employs over 100 people and manages assets totalling EUR 5 billion.
 



  





 As the company evolves, we are now looking for a Tech & AI Solutions Lead to strengthen the way technology supports our business. Together with our Transformation Lead, you will become the core of Areim's innovation hub, with responsibilities spanning from our data platform to AI and automation initiatives and the overall systems perspective.
 



  





**About the role** 




 As Tech & AI Solutions Lead, you will play a key role in Areim Forward Studio, understanding our system landscape, and seeing how different solutions affect the business and create value over time. You work closely and in continuous dialogue with the business, management and external partners across various initiatives.
 



  





 Among your responsibilities, you will define the requirements and drive development of our data platform, which is already underway, while gradually building up more knowledge and ownership within Areim. You drive AI and automation initiatives across the entire business, applying your technical expertise to evaluate, test and prioritise opportunities. Today we have no internal development team; technical implementation is largely carried out together with external consultants and partners.
 



  





 In this role, you will:
 



  





 • Take overall technical ownership of our system landscape and contribute to decisions on what we should buy, build or simplify
 



 • Define requirements, challenge and validate solution proposals from external consultants and suppliers
 



 • Establish principles for architecture, integrations, governance and security
 



 • Co-lead our AI and automation forum with our Transformation Lead
 



  





**Who we are looking for** 




 You have at least 5 years of experience within software, platform or data engineering or similar, and have since grown into a broader technical role with involvement in AI and automation initiatives. You are motivated by working closely with the business and delivering solutions that create real impact.
 



  





 Furthermore, we are looking for someone who:
 



  





 • Has a good understanding of solution architecture, integrations, data management and governance in a larger system landscape
 



 • Has been involved in building a modern data platform, either hands-on or as the person technically responsible
 



 • Has experience of AI and automation: assessing use cases, understanding technical possibilities and limitations, testing solutions and driving implementation
 



 • Has experience of managing and overseeing external suppliers and consultants
 



 • Speaks and writes Swedish and English fluently
 



  





**Meriting:** 




 • Experience in finance, asset management, real estate or another capital-intensive environment
 



 • Experience with Microsoft Fabric and related tools such as Power BI and Purview
 



  





**Who you are** 




 You are a collaborative and pragmatic person who builds trust through knowledge sharing and strong relationships. Comfortable working both strategically and hands-on, you enjoy simplifying complexity and finding practical solutions that create real value.
 



  





 Curious by nature, you thrive on working across different parts of the business, driving change and exploring new ideas. Most importantly, you know how to bring people with you and believe the best results are achieved together.
 



  





**Your application** 




 Ants Tech Recruiters is supporting Areim in this recruitment process. Please apply for the position by clicking the “Apply”-button below. Contact Johanna Ottosson,
 
 johanna.ottosson@ants.se
 
 , for any questions. Selection and interviews are conducted on an ongoing basis, so please submit your application as soon as possible. During week 29-33 we will be on vacation and answer after that.
 



  





**Why Areim?** 




 At Areim, culture is something we practice, not just express. Our purpose, Make better happen, is our driving force, and our core values guide both what we do and how we do it. With curiosity, care, and unwavering commitment, we enable people, capital, assets, communities, and the future to thrive.
 



  





 In this role, you will work alongside some of the most experienced professionals in the Nordic real asset landscape and become an important part of Areim Forward Studio.
 



  





**Are you ready to Make better happen? Join us.**

title: Senior Operations Manager, Sweden
description:
**About The Role**
 Uber is looking for an exceptional operator and leader to manage the Uber Rides operations in Sweden - one of our most strategically important markets in Northern Europe.
   

  

 You will work closely with the General Manager for the Nordics as the de facto business lead for Sweden - setting the market strategy, driving growth across both sides of our platform (riders and driver partners), and building the partnerships and capabilities that will define Uber's future in Sweden.
   

  

 This role is ideal for a curious, data-driven, and commercially sharp professional who thrives at the intersection of strategy and execution - someone who can zoom out to shape a vision and zoom in to get things done.
   

  

**What You Will Do In This Role**
 Own and Drive the Sweden Business
   

  

* Serve as the primary market lead for Sweden, accountable for growth, platform health, and overall market performance.
* Co-create the annual strategy for Sweden with the Northern Europe leadership team, then translate it into clear, measurable tactics and execution plans.


 Grow and Engage the Driver Partner Community
   

  

* Design and execute driver partner acquisition, onboarding, and retention strategies using data-driven insights and creative programmes.
* Build and sustain a high-quality driver supply that meets the evolving needs of riders across Sweden.


 Grow and Engage the Rider Base
   

  

* Develop and lead conversion and retention strategies for new and existing riders.
* Identify and unlock new use cases for Uber that expand our relevance in Swedish consumers' daily lives.
* Adapt, test and put a Swedish flavour on product rollouts to ensure they resonate culturally.


 Build Strategic Partnerships
   

  

* Identify, develop, and manage strategic partnerships with both global companies and local Swedish organisations.
* Cultivate long-term relationships that strengthen Uber's value proposition and expand our reach in the market.


 Lead Complex, Cross-Functional Projects
   

  

* Take end-to-end ownership of multiple strategic projects simultaneously, managing priorities, timelines, and stakeholder communication.
* Represent Sweden's interests clearly and confidently with senior regional and global stakeholders.


 Collaborate Across the Organisation
   

  

* Work as an integrative partner with teams across policy, communications, product, marketing, legal, finance, and customer operations.
* Champion local market context within global and regional decision-making processes.


**Basic Qualifications**
 Deep Cultural and Linguistic Fluency
   

  

* Native Swedish and professional English are mandatory. Beyond translation, you must possess a deep grasp of Swedish consumer behaviour and cultural tonality to build brand trust and navigate local policy environments securely.


 Experience and Commercial Ownership
   

  

* Around 5-8 years of experience in a fast-paced environment. This could be in high-growth technology, management consulting, consumer, or marketplace environments driving measurable commercial outcomes.
* Demonstrated experience owning a P&L, commercial budget, or significant operational domain with increasing responsibility over time.
* Experience working across multiple stakeholder groups, including senior leaders.


 Marketplace Mechanics and Tech Acumen
   

  

* A genuine understanding of how high-volume, two-sided digital marketplaces operate. You must understand algorithmic efficiency, dynamic pricing and technology tools rather than traditional fleet management.


 Data-Driven Commercial Execution
   

  

* Strong analytical intuition is required. You must be highly comfortable working with data to generate commercial insights and drive decisions, leveraging analytics tools or data visualisation platforms to assess business health and prioritize initiatives.
* A history of delivering complex, ambiguous projects on time and to a high standard within matrixed corporate environments.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Strategic clarity: Ability to develop a coherent market strategy and communicate it in a way that aligns and energises teams.
* Communication and influence: Ability to communicate with clarity and adapt your style across diverse audiences, from driver partners on the ground to C-suite stakeholders globally.
* Relationship building: A natural ability to build trust and sustain meaningful partnerships, both internally and externally.
* Resilience and adaptability: Comfort with ambiguity and the ability to make sound decisions with incomplete information in a fast-moving environment.


 ~~ ~~
   

  

**Ready to Ride?**
 This isn't the kind of place where you follow a playbook - it's where you help write one. If you're driven by impact, energized by challenge, and ready to shape how the world moves - we'd love to hear from you.
   

  

 You may be eligible for bonuses, equity, and other compensation, as well as a range of benefits. Explore our benefits.
   

  

 Offices remain key to collaboration and Uber's culture. Unless approved for full remote work, employees must spend at least 50% of their time in-office. Some roles, like those at greenlight hubs, require full-time in-office presence. Ask your Recruiter for details about this role's requirements.
   

  

 Uber is proud to be an Equal Opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected Veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing this form.

title: Ledig tjänst - Key Account Development Manager (KAD)
description:
*DHL är marknadsledande inom logistikbranschen och är ”logistikbolaget för hela världen”. DHL erbjuder expertis inom internationell express-, flyg- och sjöfrakt, väg- och tågtransport, kontraktslogistik samt internationella brevtjänster. Med ett globalt nätverk som består av mer än 220 länder och territorier och med 570 000 medarbetare över hela världen erbjuds kunder högsta kvalitet kombinerat med god lokal kännedom.*
*Allt för att på bästa sätt kunna tillgodose kundens behov av tjänster som täcker hela varuflödeskedjan. DHL tar sitt sociala ansvar genom att löpande arbeta med åtgärder för att minska klimatpåverkan och genom att bidra med katastrofhantering samt utbildning.* 
*DHL Global Forwarding ingår i DHL Group.*
 Vi på DHL Global Forwarding är väldigt glada över att kunna konstatera att vi går väldigt bra och under det senaste året vunnit marknadsandelar. Härmed söker vi nu en:
   

  

**Key Account Development Manager** 
 för att kunna möta den höga och positiva efterfrågan från våra nuvarande och framtida kunder.
   

  

 Vi söker en
 **Key Account Development Manager** 
 med inriktning att bearbeta nya och potentiella kunder i Sverige. Tjänsten är placerad på vårt HQ i Stockholm alternativt Göteborg, Helsingborg eller Malmö och är en tillsvidareanställning på heltid med omgående tillträde.
   

  

**Dina arbetsuppgifter:**
 Arbeta fokuserat med att skapa tillväxt baserat på våra flyg- respektive sjö och tågprodukter samt att marknadsföra DHL-koncernen i sin helhet. Du förväntas att självständigt och aktivt arbeta med försäljning av våra tjänster och vara delaktig i hela försäljningsprocessen. I arbetsuppgifterna ingår framförallt att bearbeta nya prospektkunder, förädla/fördjupa samarbetet med befintliga kunder, skapa relationer och att komma till avslut.
   

  

**Vem är du?**
* En affärsinriktad person med både stark drivkraft och vilja att lyckas
* Bra på att skapa och bibehålla externa och interna relationer, såväl som utveckla kundrelationer
* Lyhörd och kommunikativ
* Självgående, målfokuserad och angelägen om att komma till avslut
* Bra analysförmåga för att säkerställa rätt lösning för kund
* Har flera års erfarenhet ifrån nyckelkundsbearbetning företrädelsevis flyg & sjö
* Har stort kontaktnät hos svenska företag med omfattande transportbehov
* Besitter goda kunskaper i svenska i både tal och skrift samt goda kunskaper i engelska, vilket är vårt koncernspråk


 Du får ett stort ansvar och stora möjligheter att påverka resultat och framgång. Du som blir vår nya
 **Key Account Development Manager** 
 är van att leverera resultat. Du lockas av ett arbete som ställer krav på affärsmässighet, långsiktighet, kreativitet och vilja att åstadkomma lönsamhet. Körkort är ett krav.
   

  

**Placering på vårt HQ i Stockholm, Göteborg, Helsingborg eller Malmö.**
**Intresserad?**
 Vill du veta mer om befattningen är du välkommen att maila Rickard Winberg Head of Key Account Development Management på rickard.winberg@dhl.com.
   

  

**Ansökan**
 Din ansökan - personligt brev och CV - vill vi ha senast
 **2025-** 
**09-01** 
 .

title: (Senior) Applied Scientist, Search
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

 Wolt is part of DoorDash — together we form one of the world's largest local commerce platforms, operating across DoorDash, Deliveroo, and Wolt markets in 40+ countries. Our Consumer organisation sits at the intersection of machine learning and customer experience, responsible for helping millions of customers every day find the right restaurants, dishes, and items in search, personalised recommendations, and discovery surfaces that feel intuitive and relevant. It's a domain where scale is truly global, the engineering and applied science problems are genuinely hard and scientifically interesting, and the impact on the customer experience and our business metrics is immediate and measurable.
   

  

 We’re looking for an Applied Scientist to join our Consumer org. In this role you’ll work on some of the most technically challenging ML problems across DoorDash: understanding what our customers are looking for, identifying key concepts in their queries and surfacing the most relevant results. You'll be embedded in a cross-disciplinary team of engineers, ML engineers and applied scientists with full ownership from research to production. If your expertise matches our domain and you want to work on hard problems at global scale alongside exceptional colleagues, we'd love to meet you.
   

  

 What you’ll be doing
   

  

 As an Applied Scientist in Consumer, you’ll advance the ML models and methods that power how customers across DoorDash, Deliveroo, and Wolt find and discover content. You’ll work end-to-end collaborating closely with engineers and product managers to deliver real impact.
   

  

**Day-to-day in this role you’ll:**
* Design and develop ML models for search relevance, query understanding, and ranking that operate across DoorDash, Deliveroo, and Wolt’s 40+ markets.
* Bring state-of-the-art solutions to our stack for the delight of our customers, helping the team to impact business metrics.
* Work end-to-end on ML problems: from problem framing and data analysis through model development, offline evaluation, and production monitoring.
* Collaborate with Software Engineers, ML Engineers, Product Managers, and Analysts to translate research insights into real customer impact.
* Contribute to our group-wide Applied Science community through knowledge sharing, technical reviews, and raising the bar on ML practices.


 Our humble expectations\*
   

  

* You have 4+ years of hands-on experience in applied ML with a track record of shipping models to production (a PhD in ML with applied research experience is equally welcome).
* You have solid experience in Search: query understanding, query intent prediction, or semantic search.
* You are proficient in Python and experienced with ML frameworks and large-scale data processing.
* You communicate complex technical ideas clearly and collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.
* Experience with NLP, dense retrieval, learning-to-rank, or embedding-based methods is a strong plus.


 What we offer
   

  

 In this role, you will have a direct and measurable impact on millions of customers every day. You’ll join a team of world-class applied scientists and engineers in DoorDash, Deliveroo and
   

  

 Wolt who care about both rigour and craft, tackling problems that are genuinely hard and impactful in ways that few companies can offer. Together with your lead, you will have the opportunity to create a personalised development plan to grow your strengths and to develop new capabilities.
   

  

 📍 This role can be based in one of our tech hubs in Berlin, Helsinki, or Stockholm, or you can work remotely anywhere in Finland, Sweden, Germany. We also offer relocation support to help you join us.
   

  

 Next steps\*
   

  

 Once you apply, you will go through 4 steps in our hiring pipeline:
   

  

* TA Screen: a 30-minute introductory call with one of our Talent Partners to learn about your background and tell you more about the role.
* Hiring Manager interview: a conversation covering your domain experience in Search or Personalisation, past projects, AI fluency, and ways of working. We will tell you more about the team, use cases, our tech stack, etc. and it will be your opportunity to ask questions.
* Coding & System Design: a technical session focused on ML system design and problem-solving with some of the team’s ML Engineers.
* Project / Expertise Deep Dive: an interview with the team’s Applied Scientists, going deep on your domain expertise and past projects.


 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Systems Engineer - Satellite Communications
description:
**Description**
 Welcome to SSC Space (formerly Swedish Space Corporation), the perfect workplace for those passionate about technology in general and space in particular.
   

  

 With space organizations, research institutes, institutional and commercial actors as customers, we offer specialist expertise in satellite communications, spacecraft consulting services, rocket and balloon systems, and much more.
   

  

 SSC Space also has one of the world’s largest global networks of ground stations for satellite communications. We own and operate the Esrange Space Center in Kiruna, northern Sweden, with the aim of launching satellites in the near future.
   

  

 Do you want to play a critical role in enabling communication for missions around Earth, and beyond? Join us at SSC, Space where we help the world’s spacecraft talk to Earth!
   

  

 We operate a global network of ground stations and deliver mission-critical communication services. Our services are integral to our customers’ mission success, from launch to Lunar space operations.
   

  

 We are now expanding our Systems Engineering team and looking for both experienced and junior Systems Engineers for satellite communications. If you have experience in space operations or spacecraft communications, we especially encourage you to apply.
   

  

**Requirements**
**We’re looking for candidates who:**
* Have excellent technical skills and a desire to learn new things, likely excited about the growing space industry.
* Can communicate complex technical solutions clearly and effectively.
* Are comfortable writing documentation, and following, and improving, engineering processes
* Have ability to lead own work, independently or in a team.
* Experience with both hardware and software systems is valued, as SSC Space services span across ground infrastructure, RF systems, and software-defined mission solutions.
* Hold a Bachelor's, Master's degree or PhD in electrical engineering, space technology, or similar.


**While not required, we especially value experience in:**
* Some years of experience in systems engineering including both hardware and software components.
* Spacecraft operations or spacecraft communications (TT&C, mission control systems, space-ground interfaces).
* A demonstrated drive to run projects, ideas or concepts. We’re happy to see your GitHub repos if available, or other technical projects that you have done.
* Ground station hardware, RF signal chains, modulation/coding schemes, and protocols (e.g. CCSDS).
* Documented knowledge of a major programming/scripting language and knowledge of software architectures and trends.
* Linux environments, including configuration, Docker, and networking.


**Responsibilities**
**As a Systems Engineer at SSC Space, you will:**
* Lead the development and integration of customers' space missions into our service system, ensuring they have efficient and reliable communications for successful space missions.
* Work on development projects to improve our systems, capabilities, and service offerings.
* Act as a Subject Matter Expert in discussions with customers, suppliers, and internal stakeholders.
* Drive projects, analyses, and technical investigations.
* Provide technical support to sales in bids and proposals.


**Benefits**
**We offer you**
* A unique opportunity to contribute to a wide range of projects and play an active role in both the Swedish and international space industry.
* An excellent opportunity for personal and career development.
* An exciting, international workplace characterized by an open atmosphere and proud employees all over the world.


**Additional Benefits**
* In addition to vacation and traditional public holidays, we offer extra paid leave throughout the year, such as time off between Christmas and New Year, Ascension Day, amounting to 6-11 extra days depending on the calendar.
* Wellness allowance of SEK 5,000 per year.
* Health benefits such as voluntary health insurance and regular health checks for eligible employees.
* Access to a benefit bike through a tax-efficient leasing program.
* Support for employee-driven activities that promote connection, health, and culture across teams.
* Collective Agreement, including good pensions plans, parental pay and insurance.



**Location**
 This is a full-time, permanent position based at our office in either Solna or Kiruna.
   

  

**Further information**
 For questions regarding the position, you are welcome to contact:
   

  

 Petrus Hyvönen, Head of Engineering (petrus.hyvonen@sscspace.com)
   

  

 For questions regarding employment terms or union-related matters, you are welcome to contact our union representatives:
   

  

**Sveriges Ingenjörer** 
**:** 
 SverigesIngenjorer-AF@sscspace.com
   

  

**Unionen** 
 : unionen@sscspace.com
   

  

  

  

**Next step**
 If this opportunity sparks your interest, don’t wait to apply!
   

  

 We kindly ask you to submit your CV when applying. While you are welcome to include a brief personal note or letter, our evaluation will primarily focus on your skills and qualifications as outlined in your CV, ensuring a fair and unbiased recruitment process.
   

  

 Please note that the recruitment process will be paused during the summer holiday period. We will resume this recruitment in August once all relevant stakeholders are back. This means that feedback may take longer than usual, and we appreciate your understanding and patience during the summer period.
   

  

 This is a security-classified position and will require a background check prior to employment.
   

  

 We welcome your application no later than 2026-08-17.
   

  

**We help Earth benefit from space**
 SSC Space is a leading global provider of advanced space services, with more than 50 years of experience. We help space organizations, research institutes, commercial and institutional actors from all over the world to get access to space.
   

  

 With local presence on all continents and about 800 committed employees, we offer specialist expertise in satellite communications and satellite control services, spacecraft operations, rocket and balloon systems, launch services and flight test services, as well as engineering, operations and consultancy services for space missions.
   

  

 We help Earth benefit from Space. Learn more at sscspace.com

title: Product and Technical Communications
description:
**TL;DR** 
 - Lovable ships fast. When millions of people build real businesses on your platform, both the product story and the trust story have to be airtight. You’ll own product and technical communications that makes sure this is the case: launches, trust and incident response, and the narrative that connects what we build to why it matters.
   

  

 Why Lovable?
   

  

 For decades, the ability to build software belonged to a tiny fraction of the population: the people with the credentials, the technical training, the access. Everyone else had to wait, or pay, or give up. Lovable is the counterargument. We exist for the 99%: the people with ideas who were locked out of software creation until now.
   

  

 Over 50 million projects have been built on the platform. Hundreds of thousands of people pay for it. The product handles real infrastructure, not only prototypes: authentication, databases, payments, and deployment. People run real businesses on Lovable, which means the stakes when something breaks are real too.
   

  

 The comms challenge: you need to be credible with engineers and journalists who cover AI infrastructure, while also making the product story accessible to a much broader audience, and you need to be the person the company turns to when a product incident becomes a reputational moment. The right person will find that combination energizing.
   

  

 What you’ll do
   

  

**Own trust, security, and incident communications.** 
 People build real businesses on Lovable, and their confidence in the platform is existential to ours. You’ll own Lovable’s trust narrative in earned and new media, proactively telling the story of how we think about security, privacy, reliability, and platform safety. And when something doesn’t go as planned, you’ll be the comms lead in the room, working alongside the Head of Communications to manage the external response with a cross-functional team.
   

  

**Lead product launch communications.** 
 When Lovable ships something new, you own the earned media strategy around it, including knowing the right Substacks, podcasts, and new media channels beyond traditional outlets. You’ll work closely with product marketing to shape positioning, develop press materials, pitch the story, and make sure launches land. You’ll build a repeatable launch engine that moves as fast as the product team does.
   

  

**Turn journalists and creators into builders.** 
 Some of the most powerful product coverage comes from people who’ve experienced Lovable firsthand. You’ll develop relationships with earned media and creators by getting the product into their hands, helping journalists, technical writers, and influencers build something real on Lovable so they can tell the product story from their own experience.
   

  

**Be the comms partner to our CTO and product leadership.** 
 You’ll develop spokesperson platforms for our CTO, CISO, and senior product and engineering leaders. In partnership with product marketing, you’ll prep them for media, conferences, and podcasts. You’ll help them become recognized voices defining a new product category.
   

  

**Own product-level partner integration comms.** 
 When Lovable launches a technical integration or platform capability that changes what builders can do, you own the comms. You’ll coordinate with product marketing and partnerships to shape the narrative and get it in front of the right audiences.
   

  

**Build analyst and technical influencer relationships.** 
 You’ll build relationships with the analysts, tech media, and voices that shape opinion among technical audiences. You’ll make sure Lovable shows up in the right conversations and earns positioning in the right reports.
   

  

**Ensure product news travels beyond traditional media.** 
 Product stories in 2026 don’t just live in TechCrunch. You’ll make sure product news reaches the Substacks, podcasts, technical creators, and communities where your audiences are.
   

  

 Who you are
   

  

**You’re technically fluent and genuinely curious about the product.** 
 You don’t need to be an engineer, but you need to understand how software products work, architecturally, not just conceptually. When an engineer explains a design decision, you understand why it matters and can translate it into something a reporter or analyst would care about.
   

  

**You’ve run product comms at a fast-moving technology company.** 
 You’ve lived through the chaos of a product team that ships faster than comms can keep up, and you’ve built systems to stay ahead. You know how to run a launch process that’s tight but not slow, and you know when to push for a bigger moment versus when to just get the word out.
   

  

**You have real trust and incident comms experience.** 
 You’ve worked on or alongside security, privacy, or trust and safety teams. You know the difference between proactive trust storytelling and reactive crisis management, and you’ve done both. You’ve been in the room during a product incident and know how to craft external communications that are honest, precise, and fast.
   

  

**You have strong media relationships in the tech and AI ecosystem.** 
 You know the reporters, podcasters, and analysts who cover AI, developer tools, and enterprise technology. You understand what makes them care about a product story, and you can get Lovable in front of them.
   

  

**You write clearly, concisely, and technically without being dry.** 
 Product comms doesn’t mean jargon-heavy. Your writing is precise, human, and accessible, even when the subject matter is complex or the situation is sensitive.
   

  

**You connect dots and see around corners.** 
 A feature ships, and you immediately think: which reporter is writing about this problem? What should our CTO say on social? How does this fit the narrative arc we’ve been building for the quarter? That connective instinct is what makes this role work.
   

  

**You’re hands-on, agile, and low-ego.** 
 You’ll draft the press materials, build the media list, coordinate the launch timeline, and prep the spokesperson. You thrive in environments where the plan changes and the pace is relentless.
   

  

**You have 5–8 years of experience in communications** 
 , including product launches and trust or incident communications at a technology company. You’ve run launch processes, managed incident response, and built media relationships in the tech ecosystem.
   

  

 What you need to know
   

  

 This role requires genuine product immersion. You’ll work closely with product marketing to understand what’s shipping, why it matters, and how to position it. That partnership is what lets you move fast and tell the right story at the right moment.
   

  

 Trust and incident comms are core to the role. You’ll need to be comfortable being the person who’s on call when something breaks, who can draft an incident communication at speed, and who brings the judgment to know when to say something, when to wait, and how to say it right.
   

  

 Lovable is headquartered in Stockholm, with product and engineering teams across multiple offices. Wherever you sit, you’ll need to work effectively across time zones and build relationships with the teams who ship the product you’re talking about.
   

  

 Finally: joining Lovable Comms means you’re deeply curious, data-driven, and not extremely precious about role boundaries. You find ways to work smarter with AI, to measure what matters, and to step into work that isn't technically “yours” when the moment calls for it.
   

  

**About Your Application**
 Please submit your application in English. It's our company language, so you'll be speaking lots of it if you join. We treat all candidates equally — if you're interested, please apply through our careers portal.

title: Field Sales Engineer
description:
**About This Position**
 Are you early in your career and ready to take more ownership, build customer relationships, and deepen commercial and technical skills?
   

  

 We are looking for a Field Sales Engineer with up to 3 years of professional experience to join the Adhesive Construction team. In this role, you will work with innovative adhesive technologies that help create stronger, safer, and more sustainable furniture and engineered wood products across the Nordics.
   

  

 This role offers the opportunity to take responsibility for existing customer relationships while continuing to develop through hands on experience, close collaboration with technical specialists, and direct exposure to customer decision making. It is well suited to someone ready to step up, gain autonomy, and grow in a future focused industry.
   

  

**What You´ll Do**
 This position builds on existing experience and supports continued development as a trusted partner to customers.
   

  

* Developing a strong understanding of the adhesive product portfolio and clearly communicating its value
* Managing and growing existing customer accounts by understanding customer processes, needs, and challenges
* Identifying opportunities to develop tailored adhesive solutions for customer and market requirements
* Preparing and delivering customer presentations and contributing to commercial discussions
* Spending time on site with customers to build long term relationships
* Monitoring market developments and competitor activity to identify new business opportunities
* Working towards defined sales targets and contributing to sustainable revenue growth
* Capturing and sharing customer feedback with internal teams to support continuous improvement
* Collaborating closely with application engineering, certification, and other internal teams to support technical and certification needs


**What Makes You a Good Fit**
 This role is suited to someone with a solid foundation who is ready to develop further.
   

  

* A bachelor’s or master’s degree in engineering, chemistry, science, wood technology, business, or a related field
* Between 1 and 3 years of experience in sales, technical sales, customer facing, or commercial roles, ideally in a technical or industrial environment
* Confidence communicating with customers and internal stakeholders
* A structured, proactive, and customer focused way of working
* Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with others
* Willingness to travel frequently across the region to visit customers, as well as regularly to the Düsseldorf and Stockholm headquarters
* Interest in sustainable solutions, innovation, and practical applications
* Experience using CRM systems (such as Salesforce) and standard business tools such as Excel and PowerPoint
* Fluency in Swedish and English, both spoken and written. Additional Nordic languages are an advantage


 Please submit your application in English.
   

  

**Some perks of joining Henkel**
* Diverse national and international growth opportunities
* Global wellbeing standards with health and preventive care programs
* Gender-neutral parental leave for a minimum of 8 weeks
* Employee Share Plan with voluntary investment and Henkel matching shares
* Occupational Health Checks & Private Health Insurance
* Sports compensation allowance
* Pension
* Annual Leave: 30 days plus public holidays


 At Henkel, we come from a broad range of backgrounds, perspectives, and life experiences. We believe the uniqueness of all our employees is the power in us. Become part of the team and bring your uniqueness to us! We welcome all applications across different genders, origins, cultures, religions, sexual orientations, disabilities, and generations.

title: Web Editor - Internship
description:
### **Who we are and what we believe in**

60% of B2B deals just go quiet. No answer, no explanation, the deal just dies. A sales rep spends weeks building that relationship, and then… nothing. That's the problem GetAccept was built to solve.


Our #1 AI Digital Sales Room gives buyers and sellers a shared space to collaborate, stay aligned, and move confidently toward a signed deal. Today we're the global leader in the category, used by over 5,000 companies across 50+ countries. Our marketing website is the front door to all of that, and we're looking for a **Web Editor Intern** to help us keep it sharp, fast, and constantly improving!

### **The Role**


You'll support the day-to-day operations of the GetAccept marketing website. This is a hands-on role where you'll get your feet wet across a multitude of disciplines; web development, content management, marketing automation, and UX/UI design. All at once.

**What you'll do:**

* **QA & site monitoring:** Regularly go through our pages looking for broken links, outdated copy, and things slowing the site down.
* **Localization:** Review, refine, and edit Swedish translations of English/French content so our brand voice always feels natural.
* **Building pages:** Help build, update, and launch new marketing web pages and campaign landing pages.
* **Keeping our assets organized:** Keep our Figma files, component libraries, image banks, and other asset repositories clean and organized.
* **AI workflow ideas:** Join brainstorms on how AI could take some repetitive work off the marketing team's plate.


The internship is based at our HQ in Malmö and takes place during the internship/LIA period that is part of your education during the fall in 2026.

### **Your Team & Culture**


You'll join our Marketing team located in Sweden, UK, Spain and France! You'll find an environment that balances real ownership with real support, whether that's a quick Slack thread untangling a Figma mess, Monday breakfasts, or celebrating a well-executed page launch together.


Read more about our Culture & employee experience here.

### **What you're walking into**

* **Real ownership, real fast:** You'll be shipping pages, fixing bugs, and shaping how thousands of visitors experience our brand.
* **A strong company culture:** Experience the power of #teamwins in an environment that celebrates inclusivity, collaboration, and fun!
* **Category leadership:** Stand at the forefront of category creation, where your role and its impact are essential.
* **Cross-functional exposure:** Work across web dev, content, design, and marketing automation; a genuine crash course in how a modern marketing engine runs.
* **A fantastic HQ:** Work from our office in Västra Hamnen, Malmö, right by the sea, where we offer snacks and beverages throughout the day and access to an in-building gym.
* **Great perks:** Monday breakfasts, Friday fikas and excellent team celebrations!
* Read more about our Culture & employee experience here.

### **Who you are**

You notice the small stuff other people scroll past; a misaligned button, an old stat, a sentence that just reads a bit off. When you spot a problem you also think about how to fix it, and you like being trusted to just get on with things. You're curious about AI and automation, not because it's a buzzword, but because you enjoy figuring out how to make work easier!

### **What we're looking for**

* You know how the web actually works; CMS architecture, asset optimization, that kind of thing. You should be comfortable opening browser dev tools to check what's slow or broken.
* Fluent Swedish, written and spoken; you'll often be the last person checking Swedish content before it's published.
* Knowledge of Figma, especially keeping components and documentation tidy.
* Hands-on experience with a CMS like WordPress or Webflow.

**Nice to have:**

* Some HTML, CSS, or JavaScript.
